disclaimer: Expense ratios sourced from issuer prospectus summaries and published fund pages as of late 2024. Always verify against the most recent fund prospectus before relying on a figure. If a fund on the user's statement is not in this lookup, Fidelio must report 'expense ratio not available from this source' rather than estimate.

VTSAX.name: Vanguard Total Stock Market Index Admiral
VTSAX.plain_english: A low-cost fund that owns a tiny slice of roughly 4,000 publicly traded US companies — small, medium, and large.

VFIAX.name: Vanguard 500 Index Admiral
VFIAX.plain_english: Tracks the S&P 500 — the 500 largest US companies. One of the cheapest mainstream index funds in existence.

VTIAX.name: Vanguard Total International Stock Index Admiral
VTIAX.plain_english: Owns a slice of thousands of non-US companies — Europe, Japan, emerging markets. The international counterpart to VTSAX.

VBTLX.name: Vanguard Total Bond Market Index Admiral
VBTLX.plain_english: Owns thousands of US bonds — Treasuries, investment-grade corporates, and mortgage bonds. The standard low-cost bond anchor.

VTTSX.name: Vanguard Target Retirement 2060 Fund
VTTSX.plain_english: A pre-mixed retirement fund for someone retiring around 2060. Under the hood it's stocks + bonds that gradually shift toward bonds as 2060 approaches.

VFFVX.name: Vanguard Target Retirement 2055 Fund
VFFVX.plain_english: Pre-mixed retirement fund aimed at retiring around 2055 — mostly global stocks now, gradually shifting toward bonds.

VFIFX.name: Vanguard Target Retirement 2050 Fund
VFIFX.plain_english: Pre-mixed retirement fund aimed at retiring around 2050.

VFORX.name: Vanguard Target Retirement 2040 Fund
VFORX.plain_english: Pre-mixed retirement fund aimed at retiring around 2040 — more bonds than the later-dated funds.

VTHRX.name: Vanguard Target Retirement 2030 Fund
VTHRX.plain_english: Pre-mixed retirement fund aimed at retiring around 2030 — roughly 70% stocks, 30% bonds.

FXAIX.name: Fidelity 500 Index Fund
FXAIX.plain_english: Fidelity's S&P 500 index fund — as close to free as a mutual fund gets. Tracks the 500 largest US companies.

FSKAX.name: Fidelity Total Market Index Fund
FSKAX.plain_english: Owns essentially every US stock — mirrors VTSAX at a slightly lower price.

FTIHX.name: Fidelity Total International Index Fund
FTIHX.plain_english: International-stock counterpart to FSKAX — thousands of companies outside the US.

FXNAX.name: Fidelity US Bond Index Fund
FXNAX.plain_english: Low-cost broad US bond index — Treasuries and investment-grade corporates.

FIPFX.name: Fidelity Freedom Index 2055 Fund
FIPFX.plain_english: Fidelity's index-based target-date fund for 2055 retirement — cheap for an all-in-one retirement fund.

FFLEX.name: Fidelity Freedom 2055 Fund (Active)
FFLEX.plain_english: Fidelity's actively managed 2055 fund. Roughly 6x more expensive than the Index version for similar returns — check your statement carefully: the plan often offers both and the 'Index' version is almost always the better pick.

SPY.name: SPDR S&P 500 ETF Trust
SPY.plain_english: The original S&P 500 ETF. Cheap, but Vanguard's VOO is cheaper for the same thing.

VOO.name: Vanguard S&P 500 ETF
VOO.plain_english: Vanguard's S&P 500 ETF — tracks the 500 largest US companies at nearly zero cost.

VTI.name: Vanguard Total Stock Market ETF
VTI.plain_english: ETF version of VTSAX — owns essentially every public US company for a rounding-error expense ratio.

BND.name: Vanguard Total Bond Market ETF
BND.plain_english: ETF version of VBTLX — broad US bond exposure at near-zero cost.

AGG.name: iShares Core US Aggregate Bond ETF
AGG.plain_english: Broad US bond ETF — competitor to BND, same job, same price.

QQQ.name: Invesco QQQ Trust
QQQ.plain_english: Tracks the Nasdaq-100 — 100 largest non-financial Nasdaq companies, tech-heavy. More expensive than broad index funds because it's narrower.

AAVTX.name: American Funds 2065 Target Date Retirement R-6
AAVTX.plain_english: Actively managed target-date fund for 2065 retirement. Roughly 5x the cost of Vanguard's equivalent (VLXVX) for comparable long-run returns.

AFTLX.name: American Funds 2060 Target Date Retirement R-6
AFTLX.plain_english: Actively managed target-date fund for 2060 retirement — much more expensive than an index TDF for similar exposure.

TRRLX.name: T. Rowe Price Retirement 2060 Fund
TRRLX.plain_english: Actively managed target-date fund for 2060. Roughly 7–8x the cost of an index-based TDF — hard to justify for a typical retirement saver.

JFBRX.name: JPMorgan SmartRetirement 2060 Fund R6
JFBRX.plain_english: JPMorgan's actively managed 2060 target-date fund — mid-cost among active TDFs, still several times pricier than an index TDF.

VMFXX.name: Vanguard Federal Money Market Fund
VMFXX.plain_english: A money-market fund — very low risk, tracks short-term government rates. Fine as a cash sleeve; not a long-term investment.

VLXVX.name: Vanguard Target Retirement 2065 Fund
VLXVX.plain_english: Pre-mixed retirement fund aimed at retiring around 2065 — the latest-dated Vanguard TDF currently offered.
